# Phase A Synthesis — setup.ocx.sh Re-enablement Design Input

Author: synthesis lead, Phase A. Status: grounded design input for Phase B. Code is NOT proposed here.

Source-of-truth installers (canon):
- `/home/mherwig/dev/ocx-sion/website/src/public/install.sh` (1136 lines, verified)
- `/home/mherwig/dev/ocx-sion/website/src/public/install.ps1` (601 lines, verified)

Repo installers under re-enablement:
- `/home/mherwig/dev/setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh` (700 lines, verified)
- `/home/mherwig/dev/setup.ocx.sh/pwsh/install.ps1` (515 lines, verified)

Canonical CLI ground truth: `ocx` 0.3.1, registry `ocx.sh`, home `/home/mherwig/.ocx`, binary at `/home/mherwig/.ocx/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/cli/current/content/bin/ocx` (verified: that path exists; `.../ocx/current` does NOT exist).

---

## 0. The framing inversion (read this first)

The 9 findings unanimously and correctly invert the task's implicit framing. The valuable, deliberate engineering — the Bazelisk-style mirror knobs, the numbered exit-code contract (0–7), the clean-stdout/print-path discipline, the skip-bootstrap air-gap path — lives **only in the repo installers**, not in canon. Canon (`ocx-sion`) has none of those: `err()` always `exit 1` (verified: `install.sh:28-32` is `say "error: $1" >&2; exit 1`), `say()` writes to stdout, there are no `OCX_INSTALL_*` knobs.

Conversely, the repo installers are **flatly wrong against the real `ocx` CLI** on every runtime-contact point: bootstrap command, package id, on-disk path layout, env-file activation commands, and the verify hint. Canon is correct on all of those.

So Phase B is a **merge**, not a sync: keep the repo's value-add envelope (knobs, exit codes, stdout discipline, skip-bootstrap), and replace its broken CLI-contact core + security regressions with canon's. "Treat canon as source of truth" means *canon defines the runtime/CLI/security contract*; it does NOT mean discard the repo's knob/exit-code/stdout layer.

Confidence: high. Every load-bearing fact below was re-verified against the live CLI and on-disk files in this pass; contradictory or low-evidence claims were demoted (see §9).

---

## 1. Current state (verified)

| Artifact | State |
|---|---|
| Git history | One commit `1bf221a`, **zero tags** (verified: `git tag --list` empty). No `v1.0.0`, no release ever. |
| `ocx.lock` | **Untracked** (`?? ocx.lock`); `.ocx/` is gitignored (`.gitignore:11`). |
| Pester suite | Only `tests/install/ps1/Knobs.Tests.ps1` exists. No `ExitCodes.Tests.ps1` / `PrintPath.Tests.ps1`. |
| `ocx.toml` HEAD | `[tools]` = shellcheck, shfmt, bats; `[group.ci]` = hawkeye, actionlint, lychee, git-cliff. **No go-task.** |
| `ocx.toml` working tree | Stripped to shfmt + shellcheck, `[group.ci]` removed. |
| CI | Never invokes `task` or `ocx`; every tool installed ad-hoc via marketplace actions / apt. |
| Taskfiles | Invoke tools via `ocx exec dev.ocx.sh/<tool>` — wrong verb AND wrong registry. |

---

## 2. Source-of-truth deltas: repo installers vs canon

### 2.1 sh/install.sh deltas (all verified)

| Aspect | Repo (`setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh`) | Canon (`ocx-sion/.../install.sh`) | Reality verdict |
|---|---|---|---|
| Bootstrap cmd | `ocx --remote install --select "ocx.sh/ocx:$_version"` (`:469`) | `ocx --remote package install --select "ocx.sh/ocx/cli:$_version"` (`:830`) | **Canon correct.** Real CLI has NO `install` subcommand (verified: `ocx install` → "unrecognized subcommand 'install'", tip: 'init'). `--select` is a boolean flag; package id is positional. Package is `ocx.sh/ocx/cli`. |
| Bin path | `${OCX_HOME}/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/current/bin` (`:369,370,693`...) | `${OCX_HOME}/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/cli/current/content/bin` (`:407`...) | **Canon correct.** Verified on disk: canon path exists; repo path does not. |
| Env activation | `ocx --offline shell profile load` + `ocx --offline shell completion` (`:372-373,393-394`) | `ocx self activate --shell=<shell>` (`:409,438`...) | **Canon correct.** `ocx shell` has only `completion`/`help`; `shell profile load` errors. `self activate` verified present. |
| Env file name | extensionless `$OCX_HOME/env` (`:363`) | `$OCX_HOME/env.sh` + per-family files; **deletes** legacy `$OCX_HOME/env` via `remove_legacy_env_file` (`:544`) | **Canon correct.** Live `~/.ocx/env.sh` matches canon's shim shape exactly (verified header + `self activate --shell=sh`). |
| Verify hint | `ocx info` (`:516`) | `ocx about` (`:860`) | **Canon correct.** `ocx info` is not top-level (`info` is under `ocx package info`); `ocx about` verified. |
| Profile targeting | single file (bash → `.bash_profile`/`.profile`; zsh → `.zshenv`); no markers, no legacy migration | login + interactive (bash → `+.bashrc`; zsh → `.zprofile`+`.zshrc`, ZDOTDIR guard); `# BEGIN ocx`/`# END ocx` block; legacy-line migration; fish/nu/elvish branches | **Canon richer.** Repo writes `.zshenv` (canon strips legacy lines from it). |
| Shell coverage | sh `env` + fish only (and the fish path calls deleted subcommands) | fish/nu/elvish/powershell env families end-to-end | **Canon richer.** |
| Download hardening | curl `--tlsv1.2` only; bare `wget -q`; token inline via `-H` (argv leak); bare `tar xf` | curl `--proto '=https'`; wget TLS fail-closed gate; token via 0600 temp header-file; two-pass tar path-traversal/symlink pre-scan; `--no-same-owner --no-same-permissions --no-overwrite-dir` | **Canon hardened; repo regressed.** Security-relevant. |
| `OCX_HOME` validation | none (`:574` raw default) | absolute-path + reject `..` + reject shell metachars (`:926-944`) | **Canon hardened.** Matters because repo embeds `OCX_HOME` into env files AND now accepts CI-supplied mirror config. |
| Checksum match | loose `grep -F "$_file"` (`:310`) | exact awk `$2==file` strip leading `*` (`:317-323`) | **Canon correct** (substring-collision safe). Confidence: medium per finding 1. |

### 2.2 pwsh/install.ps1 deltas (verified line-counts; details from finding 2)

The two ps1 files are **different generations**, mirroring the sh split:
- **Repo ps1 is the v2-contract one**: `[Console]::Error` stderr logging, exit codes 2–7, full `OCX_INSTALL_*` knobs, `[switch]` params, URL templating, `-PrintPath`, skip-bootstrap path (`Install-WithoutBootstrap` via `mklink /J`).
- **Canon ps1 is pre-v2**: `Write-Host` (stdout), `exit 1` everywhere, zero knobs, no `param()` block (reads `$Version` from caller scope), mandatory bootstrap.

But canon ps1 carries security/correctness the repo lacks: `Expand-ZipSafely` (zip-slip guard) vs repo's plain `Expand-Archive`; `Assert-SafeOcxHome`; `Remove-OcxProfileLines` block migration; OrdinalIgnoreCase PATH-shadow check; runtime `$PSVersionTable` guard (`:426-435`) absent in repo. Same critical runtime divergence as sh: canon `ocx --remote package install ... ocx.sh/ocx/cli` into `...\ocx\cli\current\content\bin`; repo `ocx --remote install ... ocx.sh/ocx` into `...\ocx\current\bin`.

Both ps1 are **Windows-only** (no `$IsLinux`/`$IsMacOS`, `*-pc-windows-msvc` targets, `ocx.exe`). Per CLAUDE.md that is intended — document it explicitly rather than adding Linux-pwsh.

**Parity consequence:** the repo's sh and repo's ps1 are roughly in sync with *each other* on the v2 envelope; both are out of sync with canon on the runtime/security core. Phase B must fix sh and ps1 together (cross-installer parity rule).

---

## 3. Real OCX bootstrap / activation model (canonical)

There is **no `ocx install` and no `ocx --remote install`** (both verified to error). The real model an installer must reproduce:

1. **Download** the cargo-dist archive `ocx-<target>.tar.xz` + `sha256.sum` from the release host, verify sha256, harden-extract.
2. **Place** the binary at `$OCX_HOME/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/cli/current/content/bin/ocx` (the registry package id for the CLI is `ocx.sh/ocx/cli`, verified in `index catalog`).
3. **Bootstrap (default, networked):** `ocx --remote package install --select "ocx.sh/ocx/cli:$version"` — `--select`/`-s` is a boolean "set as current" flag (verified `ocx package install --help`), packages are positional.
4. **Write** the managed shim `$OCX_HOME/env.sh` (+ `env.fish`/`env.ps1`/`env.nu`/`env.elv`) that, at shell startup, runs `eval "$(ocx self activate --shell=sh)"` guarded by `_OCX_ENV_LOADED`. (Verified against live `~/.ocx/env.sh`.)
5. **`ocx self activate --shell=NAME`** emits: prepend `.../cli/current/content/bin` to PATH; inject completions unless `OCX_NO_COMPLETIONS=1`; `eval "$(ocx --global env --shell=sh)"`; set `OCX_ACTIVATED=1` (idempotent). Accepted shells: ash, ksh, dash, bash, elvish, fish, batch, powershell, zsh, nushell (sh ≡ dash).
6. **Optionally** add the `env.sh` source line to login + interactive profiles (canon writes BOTH).

**Skip-bootstrap reframing (open decision, see §10):** since there is no `ocx --remote install` bootstrap step to skip, "skip bootstrap" really means "drop binary + write env.sh, do NOT run the networked `ocx package install` that pulls the CLI into the package store." The repo's hand-rolled skip layout `.../ocx/$version/bin` cannot be resolved by the real `ocx` self-resolution (which expects `.../cli/current/content/bin` with `current` → `packages/ocx.sh/sha256/...`). Phase B must either (a) reproduce the real `cli/current/content/bin` + candidate/current scheme, or (b) define skip-bootstrap as binary-on-PATH-only for wrappers that never invoke `ocx self`-resolution.

**Completion shell set is narrower than activation:** `ocx shell completion --shell` accepts ONLY {bash, elvish, fish, powershell, zsh} (verified). An installer injecting completions must restrict to those five even though `self activate`/`env` accept ten.

---

## 4. The Bazelisk base-URL / format-URL knob contract (repo-only)

This is a genuine repo value-add absent from canon. Verified in `sh/install.sh:31-52` and `:136 format_url()`. Placeholders `{version}`, `{tag}`, `{target}`, `{ext}` substituted via `sed`. `{tag}` literals are built through intermediate vars to dodge the shell brace-default parser. Defaults preserve current GitHub behavior (default REPO `ocx-sh/ocx`, BASE_URL `https://github.com/${REPO}/releases/download`).

Phase B decision: keep these knobs as setup.ocx.sh-specific value-add (recommended), and consider upstreaming into canon so the two scripts stop drifting. The `pwsh/install.ps1` mirrors them via `Format-OcxUrl` (env-wins-over-switch). `OCX_INSTALL_DOWNLOADER` has no ps1 equivalent by design (pwsh always uses `Invoke-WebRequest`). See the `knobContract` field for the full table.

Important coupling: because these knobs let CI inject arbitrary mirror URLs and the installer embeds `OCX_HOME` into generated env files, the canon `OCX_HOME` validation (absolute, no `..`, no metachars) becomes MORE important in the repo than in canon. Port it.

---

## 5. Exit-code contract

Repo defines 0–7 (verified `err()` = `printf ... >&2; exit "${2:-1}"` at `sh/install.sh:74-78`); canon collapses everything to `exit 1`. The repo contract is the value-add to keep. Reality note: there is no `ocx install`, so the **code-6 label "Bootstrap (`ocx --remote install`) failure"** is doubly wrong — both the command name AND it must become `ocx --remote package install`. See `exitCodes` field. All 8 codes are real in the repo script today (each has triggering call-sites per finding 1); the prose docs that *describe* code 6 reference a non-existent command and must be corrected.

---

## 6. Shell support matrix

The installer is **always run through a POSIX `sh`**, never the user's shell-under-test (verified: bats `run sh "$INSTALL_SH"`, docker `sh /work/install.sh`). So "which shell" splits into two orthogonal questions captured in the `shellMatrix` field:
- **canInterpretPosix = YES** (sh, dash, bash, zsh, ksh): can directly interpret `install.sh` (`#!/bin/sh`, no bashisms, only `local` builtin — verified 21 uses, family-universal).
- **canInterpretPosix = NO** (fish, nu, elvish, powershell): cannot parse POSIX syntax; they consume the binary after it's on PATH (Windows → `install.ps1`; Unix fish/nu/elvish users still run `install.sh` via `sh`). Only activation must work in them.

Completion-capable shells (full `self activate` completion block): bash, zsh, fish, elvish, powershell, nushell — but `ocx shell completion` only emits for the five non-nushell ones; nushell completions come through `self activate` only.

Distro packaging reality (medium confidence, web-sourced): bash/zsh/fish packaged in all three distros; dash is Debian/Ubuntu-native (Alpine = busybox ash, Fedora has no dash pkg); ksh via loksh/mksh (Alpine), ksh (Fedora), ksh/mksh (Ubuntu universe); elvish in Alpine community + Ubuntu universe + Fedora; **nushell in NO official distro repo** (Gemfury repo or GitHub release tarball); **powershell-on-Linux via Microsoft repo or GitHub release**.

---

## 7. Docker harness redesign

Current harness (verified): `tests/docker/run.sh` validates exactly alpine/fedora/ubuntu × linux/amd64+linux/arm64 (6 cells, fail-fast:false in `test-docker-matrix.yml`); builds via `docker buildx build --platform`; entrypoint `smoke.sh` runs `OCX_INSTALL_PRINT_PATH=1 OCX_INSTALL_QUIET=1 sh /work/install.sh | tail -n1`. CLAUDE.md's matrix claim is **accurate** (this is the one major prose claim that checks out). The installer is COPYed in, not bind-mounted (minor comment drift).

Two gaps to close in Phase B:
1. **No shell axis.** Every cell runs under `/bin/sh`; the `$SHELL`-dependent profile code (`detect_profile`/`modify_shell_profile`/`create_fish_config`) is **never exercised** because every Dockerfile bakes `OCX_NO_MODIFY_PATH=1` (verified). Add a SHELL axis (shell × distro × arch) with on-demand shell provisioning (apt/dnf/apk for dash/ksh/fish/elvish; pinned GitHub-release tarball for nu and powershell). Parameterize `smoke.sh` to run the installer under the target shell AND export `SHELL=`, and override `OCX_NO_MODIFY_PATH` so the profile path is tested; assert the expected profile artifact + the `.ocx/env.sh` source line per shell. Gate the full cross-product (up to 7×3×2=42) behind nightly/dispatch; run a reduced PR subset.
2. **The harness exercises a broken installer.** Once §3 fixes land, the docker assertions must check `env.sh` (not extensionless `env`), the `cli/current/content/bin` path, and `ocx about`/`ocx version` (not `ocx info`).

Highest-value new cell: alpine (musl) + {bash, zsh, fish, elvish, nu} activation.

---

## 8. Task + direnv toolchain plan

direnv mechanism is sound: `.envrc` runs `watch_file ocx.toml ocx.lock` + `eval "$(ocx direnv export)"` (both real subcommands, verified). But the taskfiles are pervasively broken (verified):

1. **`ocx exec` does not exist** — the executor is `ocx run` (verified: `ocx exec` → "unrecognized subcommand 'exec'"; ocx 0.3.1 help text still references a phantom `ocx exec` — an upstream doc bug; do NOT let it drive taskfiles back to `exec`). Every `ocx exec dev.ocx.sh/<tool> -- <tool>` call fails immediately.
2. **`ocx run` takes binding NAMES, not package refs** (verified: `ocx run ocx.sh/shfmt:3 -- ...` → "binding ... not found in selected groups"; `ocx run shfmt -- shfmt --version` → v3.13.1 works). Tools must be declared in `ocx.toml [tools]` then invoked by binding name.
3. **Wrong registry**: taskfiles use `dev.ocx.sh/<tool>`; that registry only hosts `ocx/cli` + `ocx/mirror` (verified `index catalog dev.ocx.sh`). Tools live under `ocx.sh/`.
4. **Hallucinated packages**: bats, hawkeye, actionlint exist in **neither** registry (verified: each "not found in the index"). The full reachable `ocx.sh` catalog is: bun, cpython, git-cliff, go-task, lychee, nodejs, ocx/cli, shellcheck, shfmt, uv. (Note: finding 6's claim that HEAD `ocx.toml` represents a resolvable toolchain is WRONG — its `bats`/`hawkeye`/`actionlint` entries are unresolvable; finding 9 wins.)

Phase B plan:
- Rewrite `ocx exec <PKG> -- <TOOL>` → declare in `ocx.toml [tools]`, invoke `ocx run <name> -- <binary>`. Drop PACKAGE from the run call (it belongs only in `ocx.toml`).
- Fix registry prefix `dev.ocx.sh/` → `ocx.sh/` everywhere.
- Recommended resolvable `[tools]`: `task = ocx.sh/go-task:3` (dogfood the runner — note go-task is NOT in HEAD ocx.toml and must be added), `shfmt = ocx.sh/shfmt:3`, `shellcheck = ocx.sh/shellcheck:0.11`, `git-cliff = ocx.sh/git-cliff:2`, `lychee = ocx.sh/lychee:0`; optionally `python3 = ocx.sh/cpython:3` for the bats fixture server.
- bats/hawkeye/actionlint: keep system/CI-provided behind `command -v` preconditions (the pattern pwsh/docker taskfiles already use) or drop the gate. They are NOT OCX-provisionable today.
- pwsh, docker, rsync, ssh, tar, xz, sha256sum: stay system tools (not in registry).
- Fix `ocx:index-update` arg list to `ocx.sh/` + only the four real packages (+ go-task).
- Re-run `ocx lock` after expanding `[tools]` (current lock pins only shfmt+shellcheck) and commit `ocx.lock`.

---

## 9. setup-ocx dogfood plan (CI)

The `ocx-sh/setup-ocx` action (node24 JS, `/home/mherwig/dev/setup-ocx`) installs the `ocx` binary and, in project mode (default when `ocx.toml` present), runs `ocx --project <file> pull` then parses `ocx --project <file> env --shell=bash` output, replaying via `core.addPath`/`core.exportVariable`. It caches binary + object store keyed on `sha256(ocx.lock)`.

Current setup.ocx.sh CI uses **none** of this — every tool is ad-hoc (mfinelli/setup-shfmt, raven-actions/actionlint, korandoru/hawkeye, lycheeverse/lychee-action, apt bats, orhun/git-cliff-action) and `task`/`ocx` are never invoked. So CLAUDE.md's "Tools come from OCX" is aspirational, not real.

Blockers (must land first, all verified):
1. **Commit `ocx.lock`** — project mode hard-requires it (`src/project.ts:56-61` warns + `ocx pull` fails otherwise). Currently untracked.
2. **Registry reconciliation** — `ocx.toml`/`ocx.lock` use `ocx.sh/`, taskfiles use `dev.ocx.sh/`; they must agree (see §8).
3. **Add go-task** to `ocx.toml` so the action provisions `task` itself.
4. **Restore the full toolchain** in `ocx.toml` (do NOT land the working-tree diff stripping it to shfmt+shellcheck) — but **only with packages that actually resolve** (so bats/hawkeye/actionlint stay marketplace/system, NOT in `[tools]`).

Rollout: land blockers 1–4 in one PR, prove `task verify` runs locally, then convert `verify.yml`/`test-installers.yml` to `uses: ocx-sh/setup-ocx@<SHA>` + `task ...` in a follow-up (SHA-pinned per repo convention). The docker-matrix workflow needs only QEMU+Buildx; setup-ocx optional there.

Open: whether `dev.ocx.sh` is a reachable public index or an auth-gated private one (its `ocx/mirror` entry hints at a planned tool mirror). The whole dogfood plan hinges on the canonical-registry decision (§10).

---

## 10. GitLab Function / OCI packaging path

GitLab "CI/CD Functions" (docs.gitlab.com/ci/functions/, renamed from "CI/CD Steps") is **real but experimental**, distinct from CI/CD Components/Catalog. A Function = directory with `func.yml` (two YAML docs: `spec` inputs/outputs + an `exec`/`run` definition) + scripts, distributed primarily as an OCI image. Invoked via the `run:` keyword with `func:` steps (cannot coexist with `before_script`/`script`/`after_script`); the `exec.command` form can invoke an arbitrary script.

Recommended design (finding 8, no hallucinations flagged): wrap the existing `install.sh` via `func.yml` `exec.command: ["${{ func_dir }}/install.sh"]`, map `OCX_INSTALL_*` knobs to `spec.inputs` → env, capture the `OCX_INSTALL_PRINT_PATH=1` final stdout line as a function output (`bin_dir`). Build/publish via GitLab's **built-in** `builtin://function/oci/build` + `builtin://function/oci/publish` (not `ocx package push`, whose OCI artifacts are `type=bundle` and semantically a tool-bundle, not a GitLab function image). Requires GitLab Runner 18.9+ for OCI loading. Ship in a separate repo (consistent with CLAUDE.md noting the GLF lives in its own repo); keep rsync-to-setup.ocx.sh as the stable default until Functions reaches GA. `ocx package push` is feasible only for the orthogonal future of publishing the install *scripts* as a generic OCI artifact for non-GitLab consumers.

---

## 11. Reconciled contradictions (which finding won, and why)

- **HEAD ocx.toml toolchain**: finding 6 treated HEAD `[tools]` (incl. bats) + `[group.ci]` (hawkeye/actionlint) as "the full resolvable toolchain." Finding 9 proved bats/hawkeye/actionlint resolve in NO registry. **Finding 9 wins** (verified offline catalog). Implication: HEAD `ocx.toml` itself contains unresolvable packages; the "restore full toolchain" recommendation must be scoped to resolvable packages only.
- **go-task in ocx.toml**: finding 6 implied go-task is in HEAD `[tools]`. Verified FALSE (HEAD has shellcheck/shfmt/bats only); go-task is in *setup-ocx's* own ocx.toml. Phase B must ADD go-task.
- **Which ps1 is canonical**: findings 1/2/4 agree the labelled "source-of-truth" ps1 is the OLDER generation while the repo ps1 carries the v2 contract. Reconciled in §0/§2.2: canon owns runtime/security core, repo owns the v2 envelope; merge.
- **`ocx exec` existence**: finding 9 (verified absent) overrides the stale ocx 0.3.1 help text that still mentions `ocx exec`.

---

## 12. Phase B sequencing (suggested)

1. Merge canon's runtime/security core into repo `sh/install.sh` + `pwsh/install.ps1` (bootstrap cmd, `ocx.sh/ocx/cli`, `cli/current/content/bin`, `self activate` env shims, `env.sh` + legacy cleanup, `ocx about`, download/extract hardening, `OCX_HOME` validation, zip-slip/`$PSVersionTable` for ps1) while keeping the repo's knobs/exit-codes/stdout/skip-bootstrap envelope. Mirror in both installers same PR.
2. Update bats + add the two missing Pester suites to assert the new reality (env.sh, real path, `ocx about`, activation).
3. Fix prose (CLAUDE.md, README.md, .claude/rules/installers.md): bootstrap command, exit-code-6 label, the fictional v1.0.0/"post-1.0" framing, the overstated Pester parity, the wrong README Windows `-Version` one-liner.
4. Fix taskfiles (`ocx run`, `ocx.sh/`, drop hallucinated tools), add go-task, restore+rescope ocx.toml, commit `ocx.lock`.
5. Add docker SHELL axis.
6. Dogfood setup-ocx in CI.
7. (Separate repo) GitLab Function wrapping install.sh.

---

## Appendix A — Hallucination ledger

| Severity | Location | Repo claim | Reality |
|---|---|---|---|
| CRITICAL | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:469 bootstrap_ocx() and pwsh/install.ps1 (bootstrap) + CLAUDE.md/README.md/.claude/rules/installers.md | Bootstrap via `ocx --remote install --select "ocx.sh/ocx:$version"` | Real ocx 0.3.1 has NO `install` subcommand (verified: `ocx install`/`ocx --remote install` → 'unrecognized subcommand install', tip: init). Correct form is `ocx --remote package install --select "ocx.sh/ocx/cli:$version"` — package id is `ocx.sh/ocx/cli`, `--select` is a boolean flag, package is positional. Canon uses the correct form. |
| CRITICAL | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:369,370,693 + pwsh/install.ps1 store paths (canonical bin path) | OCX bin dir is `${OCX_HOME}/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/current/bin` | Verified on disk: real layout is `${OCX_HOME}/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/cli/current/content/bin` (exists); `.../ocx/current` does not exist. Repo path is missing `/cli/` and `/content/`, so PATH activation, env file, GITHUB_PATH export, idempotent probe, and print-path all emit a nonexistent directory. |
| CRITICAL | taskfile.yml:100 + meta/release/test/ocx taskfiles (ocx exec) | Tools run via `ocx exec <PKG> -- <TOOL>` | `ocx exec` is not a subcommand in ocx 0.3.1 (verified → 'unrecognized subcommand exec'). The composed-env executor is `ocx run`. Every lint/test/release task fails immediately. |
| CRITICAL | taskfiles/ocx.taskfile.yml:35,45 (run with PACKAGE ref) | Executor invoked with a full package ref, e.g. `ocx run dev.ocx.sh/shfmt:3 -- shfmt` | Verified: `ocx run` accepts binding NAMES declared in ocx.toml, not package refs (`ocx run ocx.sh/shfmt:3 -- ...` → 'binding not found in selected groups'; `ocx run shfmt -- shfmt --version` → v3.13.1). Tools must be declared in [tools] then invoked by name. |
| CRITICAL | taskfile.yml + meta/release/test taskfiles (dev.ocx.sh prefix) | Tool packages live under registry `dev.ocx.sh/` (shfmt, shellcheck, bats, hawkeye, actionlint, lychee, git-cliff) | Verified: `dev.ocx.sh` only hosts ocx/cli + ocx/mirror. Tools live under `ocx.sh/`. All dev.ocx.sh/ tool refs are unresolvable. |
| HIGH | taskfile.yml + test.taskfile.yml + ocx.toml HEAD [tools] (bats) / meta.taskfile.yml (hawkeye, actionlint) | bats (`ocx.sh/bats:1.11`/`dev.ocx.sh/bats:1.11`), hawkeye (`...hawkeye:6`), actionlint (`...actionlint:1.7`) are OCX packages | Verified absent from BOTH registries ('not found in the index'). The full reachable ocx.sh catalog is bun/cpython/git-cliff/go-task/lychee/nodejs/ocx/cli/shellcheck/shfmt/uv. These three are hallucinated OCX packages; must stay system/CI-provided. |
| HIGH | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:372-373,393-394 create_env_file()/create_fish_config() | Generated env file activates ocx via `ocx --offline shell profile load` + `ocx --offline shell completion` | `ocx shell` exposes only completion/help; `ocx shell profile load` does not exist (errors). Canon uses `ocx self activate --shell=<shell>` (verified present; live ~/.ocx/env.sh confirms). Repo eval calls silently no-op (guarded `\|\| true`), leaving activation/completions unloaded. |
| HIGH | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:266-293 (token/download) + :630-632 (tar) | Downloads + extracts the archive safely | Repo dropped canon hardening: no curl `--proto '=https'`, no wget TLS fail-closed gate, GITHUB_TOKEN inline in argv (process-list leak) vs canon 0600 temp header-file, bare `tar xf` with no path-traversal/symlink pre-scan and no `--no-same-owner/--no-same-permissions/--no-overwrite-dir`. Security regressions vs canon. |
| HIGH | pwsh/install.ps1:420 (Expand-Archive) + missing Assert-SafeOcxHome / $PSVersionTable guard | Repo ps1 is the hardened v2 installer | Repo ps1 uses plain Expand-Archive (no zip-slip guard on PS<7.4), lacks Assert-SafeOcxHome OCX_HOME validation, and lacks the runtime $PSVersionTable guard canon has (:426-435). Security/correctness present in canon ps1 is absent in repo ps1. |
| HIGH | .claude/rules/workflow-release.md + CLAUDE.md release section | '≥1.0 (current state, last release v1.0.0)' — the whole post-1.0 bump regime is the current state | Verified: repo has ZERO git tags and a single bootstrap commit. There has never been a v1.0.0 or any release. The post-1.0 framing is fictional. |
| MEDIUM | setup.ocx.sh ocx.toml HEAD [tools] / taskfile.yml (go-task) | (finding 6) HEAD ocx.toml [tools] includes the runner/go-task and represents the resolvable canonical toolchain | Verified: HEAD [tools] is shellcheck/shfmt/bats only — NO go-task; and bats/hawkeye/actionlint are unresolvable. go-task must be added to dogfood `task`. Reconciled in favor of finding 9. |
| MEDIUM | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:363,435 (env file name + source line) | Writes and sources the extensionless env file `$OCX_HOME/env` | Canon writes `$OCX_HOME/env.sh` AND explicitly deletes the extensionless `$OCX_HOME/env` as known-stale (remove_legacy_env_file:544). Repo recreates exactly the file canon treats as a bug. |
| MEDIUM | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:516 print_success() + pwsh/install.ps1:342 | Verify with `ocx info` | No top-level `ocx info` (info is under `ocx package info`). Canon correctly uses `ocx about` (verified present). Users get an unknown-subcommand error. |
| MEDIUM | .claude/rules/testing-pwsh.md + CLAUDE.md (Pester parity) | Three symmetric Pester suites (Knobs/ExitCodes/PrintPath) with full Bats parity; 'every Bats test has a matching Pester test' | Verified: only Knobs.Tests.ps1 exists. No ExitCodes/PrintPath Pester suites; coverage is partial, not symmetric. |
| MEDIUM | README.md:48 (Windows pinned-version one-liner) | `& { iex "$(irm .../install.ps1)" } -Version 0.5.0` | Trailing -Version after the scriptblock does not bind to the script's caller-scope $Version fallback; pinned version is likely ignored. Correct idiom is `& { $Version = '0.5.0'; irm ... \| iex }` or `pwsh -File install.ps1 -Version 0.5.0`. |
| MEDIUM | CLAUDE.md Commands section + taskfile.yml | 'Tools come from OCX (no local install needed)'; `task verify` drives the CI quality gate | Verified: CI never invokes `task` or `ocx`; every workflow re-installs tools via marketplace actions/apt. The OCX taskfile is local-only, so CI and local toolchains can drift (e.g. shfmt 3.10.0 pinned in verify.yml vs ocx.sh/shfmt:3). |
| HIGH | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:481-490 install_without_bootstrap() | Skip-bootstrap places binary at `$OCX_HOME/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/$version/bin/ocx` with current→$version symlink | Real store layout is `.../ocx/cli/current/content/bin/ocx` (current → packages/ocx.sh/sha256/...). Repo's hand-rolled layout omits /cli/ and /content/ and cannot be resolved by real ocx self-resolution. |
| MEDIUM | setup.ocx.sh/sh/install.sh:574 + config block :31-52 (OCX_HOME) | Bazelisk-style mirror knobs are the load-bearing feature (implying they come from canon); OCX_HOME used as-is | Canon has NO such knobs and NO OCX_HOME validation; the knobs are a repo-only invention. The repo also dropped canon's OCX_HOME absolute-path/'..'/metachar guards — important since OCX_HOME is embedded into generated env files and CI can now supply mirror config. |
| LOW | tests/docker/run.sh:3 + docker.taskfile.yml:3-4 (header comments) | Harness 'mounts' / bind-mounts sh/install.sh | Installer is COPYed into the image at build time (COPY sh/install.sh /work/install.sh), not bind-mounted. Wording-only mismatch. |

---

## Appendix B — Knob contract (Bazelisk-style, repo value-add)

| Knob | sh | ps1 | Default | Purpose |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| `OCX_INSTALL_REPO` | Y | Y | `ocx-sh/ocx` | GitHub repo slug used to derive default BASE_URL/API_URL |
| `OCX_INSTALL_BASE_URL` | Y | Y | `https://github.com/${OCX_INSTALL_REPO}/releases/download` | Release-asset base URL (mirror root) |
| `OCX_INSTALL_API_URL` | Y | Y | `https://api.github.com/repos/${OCX_INSTALL_REPO}/releases` | GitHub releases API base for latest-version resolution |
| `OCX_INSTALL_FORMAT_URL` | Y | Y | `${OCX_INSTALL_BASE_URL}/{tag}/ocx-{target}.{ext}` | Archive URL template; placeholders {version} {tag} {target} {ext} |
| `OCX_INSTALL_CHECKSUM_FORMAT_URL` | Y | Y | `${OCX_INSTALL_BASE_URL}/{tag}/sha256.sum` | sha256.sum URL template; same placeholders |
| `OCX_INSTALL_SKIP_BOOTSTRAP` | Y | Y | `0` | 1 = skip the networked ocx package-install bootstrap, place binary directly (air-gapped CI) |
| `OCX_INSTALL_PRINT_PATH` | Y | Y | `0` | 1 = emit absolute bin dir as the final stdout line (downstream BIN_DIR=$(.../tail -n1)) |
| `OCX_INSTALL_FORCE` | Y | Y | `0` | 1 = reinstall even if same version already present (bypass idempotent fast-path) |
| `OCX_INSTALL_QUIET` | Y | Y | `0` | 1 = suppress informational say() output (errors still emitted) |
| `OCX_INSTALL_NO_BIN_SMOKETEST` | Y | Y | `0` | 1 = skip the post-extract `$bin version` smoke check |
| `OCX_INSTALL_DOWNLOADER` | Y | - | `(empty = auto)` | Force a specific downloader (curl/wget); sh-only — pwsh always uses Invoke-WebRequest |
| `OCX_HOME` | Y | Y | `~/.ocx ($env:USERPROFILE/.ocx on Windows)` | Install root (user-facing, survives install). Canon validates absolute/no-../no-metachars; repo currently does not |
| `OCX_NO_MODIFY_PATH` | Y | Y | `(empty/0)` | Truthy = skip shell profile modification |
| `GITHUB_TOKEN` | Y | Y | `(empty)` | GitHub API auth; canon passes via 0600 temp header-file, repo passes inline (argv leak) |
| `NO_COLOR` | Y | Y | `(empty)` | Disable bold/color in output |

---

## Appendix C — Exit codes

| Code | Real | Meaning |
|---|---|---|
| 0 | yes | Success |
| 1 | yes | Generic / legacy fallback (err default when no code given) |
| 2 | yes | Argument or environment validation failure |
| 3 | yes | Network / download / API failure |
| 4 | yes | Checksum mismatch |
| 5 | yes | Archive extraction failure |
| 6 | yes | Bootstrap failure — prose labels it 'ocx --remote install' which is wrong; the real command is 'ocx --remote package install' |
| 7 | yes | Unsupported platform / architecture |

---

## Appendix D — Shell support matrix

| Shell | POSIX-interpretable | Activation | Docker provisioning |
|---|---|---|---|
| sh (POSIX, busybox ash) | yes | env.sh sourced -> ocx self activate --shell=sh: PATH export + eval ocx --global env + OCX_ACTIVATED guard; no completion block | native /bin/sh in all distros (alpine=busybox ash, ubuntu=dash, fedora=bash-as-sh) |
| dash | yes | same as sh (sh == dash); env.sh source line via profile | Ubuntu native (apt dash); Alpine uses busybox ash; Fedora has no dash pkg |
| bash | yes | ocx self activate --shell=bash: PATH export + full _ocx()/COMPREPLY completion; sourced from login + interactive rc | apk add bash / dnf install bash / apt-get install bash (all preinstalled in repo Dockerfiles) |
| zsh | yes | ocx self activate --shell=zsh: PATH export + #compdef/_arguments completion; canon writes .zprofile + .zshrc (repo writes .zshenv) | apk add zsh / dnf install zsh / apt-get install zsh |
| ksh | yes | ocx self activate --shell=ksh: PATH-only, no completion block | apk add loksh/mksh / dnf install ksh / apt-get install ksh (mksh/ksh93 universe) |
| fish | no | ocx self activate --shell=fish: set -x PATH + fish completion; env.fish shim + ~/.config/fish/conf.d/ocx.fish; no profile-block edit | apk add fish / dnf install fish / apt-get install fish |
| nu (nushell) | no | ocx self activate --shell=nushell: $env.PATH + completion (temp-file dance, parse-time source); env.nu shim + nushell/vendor/autoload/ocx.nu; NOT in shell-completion shell set | NOT in any official distro repo; Gemfury repo or pinned GitHub release tarball |
| elvish | no | ocx self activate --shell=elvish: set E:PATH + edit:completion:arg-completer; env.elv shim + rc.elv '# BEGIN ocx / eval (slurp ...)' block | apk add elvish (community) / dnf install elvish / apt-get install elvish (universe); fallback GitHub release tarball |
| powershell | no | Windows install via install.ps1; ocx self activate --shell=powershell: $env:PATH + Register-ArgumentCompleter; env.ps1 shim (ocx.exe) | NOT in default Linux repos; Microsoft repo or GitHub release tarball; Windows ships natively |

---

## Appendix E — Open Phase-B questions (synthesis)

1. Skip-bootstrap semantics: is OCX_INSTALL_SKIP_BOOTSTRAP meant to produce a store that real `ocx` self-resolution can use (then it MUST reproduce the cli/current/content/bin + candidate/current scheme), or is it just 'binary on PATH for a wrapper that never calls ocx self-resolution'? The repo's hand-rolled .../ocx/$version/bin layout cannot work for the former.
2. Ownership of the v2 envelope: should the Bazelisk knobs + exit-code contract (0-7) + clean-stdout/print-path discipline be upstreamed into the canonical ocx-sion installer, or formally adopted as setup.ocx.sh-specific divergence? Leaving it silent guarantees continued drift.
3. Canonical registry decision: is the project's tool registry the public `ocx.sh` (so curl|sh users and CI share it) or a private `dev.ocx.sh` that must mirror the tools first (note dev.ocx.sh/ocx/mirror exists but hosts no tool packages today)? The taskfile fix, the lock contents, and the setup-ocx dogfood plan all hinge on this.
4. Hallucinated tools: are bats/hawkeye/actionlint planned ocx.sh packages (roadmap) or pure inventions? Until published they cannot be in ocx.toml [tools]; confirm whether to keep them system/CI-provided permanently or wait for packages.
5. Does the real GitHub release publish `ocx-${target}.tar.xz` + `sha256.sum` at releases/download/v${version}/, and does the cargo-dist archive contain `ocx-${target}/ocx` or a top-level `ocx`? Not verified against a live release in this read-only pass; both scripts assume specific layouts.
6. Which ocx tag stream / pin policy should the published installers target for the CLI itself (latest vs pinned minor), and pin by tag or digest? Global ocx.toml pins tools at :latest/major but that may not be CLI policy.
7. Should OCX_INSTALL_PRINT_PATH output translate to the GitLab Function `outputs` (JSON name/value to output_file) directly, or does it need a shim? Affects whether existing BIN_DIR=$(./install.sh|tail -n1) wrappers port cleanly.
8. CI minute budget for the docker SHELL axis: full shell x distro x arch is up to 42 cells; what PR-time subset vs nightly-only full grid is acceptable, and is powershell-on-Linux activation in scope or delegated entirely to a Windows Pester runner?
9. Should python3 (ocx.sh/cpython) be OCX-provisioned for the bats fixture HTTP server, or kept as system python3? Determines whether cpython belongs in ocx.toml.
10. Is the working-tree ocx.toml diff (stripping to shfmt+shellcheck, dropping [group.ci]) an intentional scope reduction or stale local experimentation to discard before dogfooding?

---

## Appendix F — Completeness critic

**Overall confidence:** medium

### Gaps

- **Real GitHub release artifact layout (synthesis Q5, treated as unknown — now resolved)**
  - Missing: The synthesis left as 'not verified against a live release' both (a) whether the release publishes sha256.sum and (b) the archive-internal layout. I verified via gh API on v0.3.1: the release publishes BOTH per-asset checksums (ocx-<target>.tar.xz.sha256, one per asset) AND an aggregate sha256.sum (803 bytes). The archive internal layout is FLAT (dist-manifest 'assets' = [CHANGELOG.md, LICENSE, README.md, ocx]; binary at archive root, NOT nested under ocx-<target>/). Consequence: both installers' default OCX_INSTALL_CHECKSUM_FORMAT_URL of '{tag}/sha256.sum' is CORRECT against reality (the synthesis implied this was a risk). And both installers already handle the flat layout via their second branch (elif [ -f "$_tmpdir/ocx" ]) — install.sh:1063-1064 and the repo's equivalent. The synthesis never resolved this and the open question framing overstated the risk.
  - Close: Treat Q5 as RESOLVED: keep sha256.sum default; keep both extraction branches (nested+flat). No code risk here. But verify the same flat layout holds for the *.zip (Windows) artifacts before trusting the ps1 Expand path — I could not download archives (egress blocked) so the .zip internal layout is inferred from dist-manifest only.
- **Minimal taskfile fix overlooked: ocx package exec takes package refs**
  - Missing: The synthesis declares 'ocx exec does not exist' and jumps straight to a [tools]+'ocx run <name>' rewrite. I verified that `ocx package exec <PKG>... -- <CMD>...` DOES exist (ocx package --help lists it; `ocx package exec ocx.sh/shfmt:3 -- shfmt --version` printed v3.13.1 and auto-installed). The current taskfiles use `ocx exec dev.ocx.sh/<pkg> -- <tool>` with package refs — so the *smallest* correct fix is `ocx exec` -> `ocx package exec` while KEEPING the package-ref form (after fixing the dev.ocx.sh -> ocx.sh registry). The synthesis's larger [tools]+ocx-run rewrite is a valid design choice, but presenting it as the only correct path is wrong; there is a lower-churn alternative the synthesis never surfaced, with different tradeoffs (package exec does not need declarations in ocx.toml, but is non-hermetic and re-resolves each call).
  - Close: Run `ocx package exec --help` and confirm. Then make an explicit decision in Phase B: package exec (ref-based, no toml decl) vs run (name-based, needs [tools]+lock). The dogfood/setup-ocx plan only needs [tools] for the *project* toolchain (`ocx pull`/`run`); CI tool execution could use either. Do not assert 'ocx run is the only fix.'
- **self activate shell enum — synthesis undercounts**
  - Missing: Synthesis §3 says 'Accepted shells: ash, ksh, dash, bash, elvish, fish, batch, powershell, zsh, nushell (sh == dash)' and elsewhere calls it ten. `ocx self activate --help` lists ELEVEN possible values: ash, ksh, dash, bash, elvish, fish, batch, powershell, zsh, nushell — and the prose legal-values line adds `sh` explicitly. The synthesis's shellMatrix omits `batch` (Windows cmd) as an activation target entirely. Minor, but the shellMatrix claims completeness it does not have.
  - Close: Add `batch` to the shell matrix as activation-capable (Windows cmd.exe), decide whether the installer should ever emit batch activation, and reconcile the 'ten' vs 'eleven' count in the design doc.
- **Bats fixtures: empty dir + nested-only layout + stub that defeats the hallucination fix**
  - Missing: Three unflagged problems. (1) tests/install/fixtures/ is EMPTY (0 files) — fixtures are generated at runtime by tests/install/helpers/server.bash:server_build_fixture, NOT static tarballs. The project rule .claude/rules/testing-bash.md AND the synthesis both describe a stale 'fixtures/ contains tarballs + sha256.sum' model that does not match reality. (2) The fixture archive is built NESTED (`tar cJf ... ocx-${target}` -> archive contains ocx-<target>/ocx), so bats only exercises the nested extraction branch; the FLAT branch that production actually hits is never covered by bats (only docker smoke hits flat, against real releases). (3) The fixture ocx stub matches only on `$1 == --remote` / `version`; it has no `package install`, `self activate`, or `about` handling and exits 0 on any `--remote`. So after Phase B corrects the bootstrap command to `ocx --remote package install --select ocx.sh/ocx/cli:$v`, the stub still passes regardless — the test gives ZERO protection against the exact hallucination Phase B is fixing.
  - Close: Update testing-bash.md to describe the runtime fixture builder. Add a flat-layout fixture variant. Rewrite the stub to assert the exact bootstrap argv (positional `ocx.sh/ocx/cli:VERSION` + `--select`, subcommand `package install`) and to emit plausible `self activate` output, so the corrected CLI-contact is actually verified, not just file placement.
- **Bats tests currently encode the WRONG path contract and pass green**
  - Missing: tests/install/env-knobs.bats:38,44,99,103,131 assert `${OCX_HOME}/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/current/bin` — the BROKEN path (missing /cli/ and /content/). These tests are GREEN today against the broken installer, so the suite actively certifies the wrong contract. The synthesis says 'update bats to assert new reality' but does not flag that the existing suite provides FALSE assurance — a reviewer trusting green CI would never see the path bug.
  - Close: When the path is corrected, flip every `ocx/current/bin` assertion to `ocx/cli/current/content/bin`. Audit all three .bats files (env-knobs, exit-codes, print-path) and the docker assertions for the old path string before merging.
- **exit-6 bootstrap test does not validate the command, only the failure surface**
  - Missing: tests/install/exit-codes.bats:80-112 builds a stub that matches `--remote) ... exit 9` and asserts the installer exits 6. It never checks WHICH command was invoked. So the test cannot distinguish `ocx --remote install` (hallucinated) from `ocx --remote package install` (correct). The exit-code contract is tested; the command correctness is not.
  - Close: Add an assertion that captures the stub's received argv (write $@ to a file in the stub) and assert it equals the corrected `package install --select ocx.sh/ocx/cli:0.0.0` form. Without this, the bootstrap-command hallucination can silently regress.
- **Entire PowerShell side is unrunnable in this environment**
  - Missing: There is NO pwsh on PATH (verified). Every ps1 claim in the synthesis — repo uses `ocx --remote install`/`ocx\current\bin`/`Expand-Archive`/`ocx info`/`shell profile load`; canon uses `cli\current\content\bin`/`Expand-ZipSafely`/`ocx about`/`self activate --shell=powershell`/$PSVersionTable guard — I confirmed only by grepping the FILES, not by executing them. `self activate --shell=powershell` is inferred-valid because the sh CLI's --shell enum includes powershell, but no ps1 script was actually run, and there are no ExitCodes/PrintPath Pester suites to run anyway. The Windows .zip archive internal layout is also unverified (egress blocked; only dist-manifest consulted).
  - Close: Phase B parity work for ps1 MUST be validated on a Windows or pwsh-on-Linux runner (CLAUDE.md says Pester runs on windows-latest + ubuntu-latest-with-pwsh). Treat all ps1 behavioral claims as static-analysis-only until a pwsh runner executes them. Confirm the *.zip flat layout before trusting Expand-ZipSafely's binary-locate logic.
- **Skip-bootstrap store layout — synthesis poses the question but cannot answer it without testing real ocx self-resolution**
  - Missing: Synthesis §3/§10/Q1 correctly flag that the repo's hand-rolled skip layout `.../ocx/$version/bin` cannot be resolved by real ocx, and that the real scheme is `.../cli/current/content/bin` with current -> packages/ocx.sh/sha256/.... But nobody has actually tested whether dropping a binary at the canonical path WITHOUT running `ocx package install` produces a self-resolvable install (i.e., whether `current` -> `packages/.../sha256/...` requires the object store to be populated, which only `package install`/`pull` does). The 'reproduce cli/current/content/bin' option (a) may be infeasible without the object-store entry.
  - Close: On a clean OCX_HOME, manually place the binary at the canonical path and run `ocx version`/`ocx self`-resolution to see if it works offline, OR inspect the live store: `ls -la ~/.ocx/symlinks/ocx.sh/ocx/cli/current` to see if `current` is a symlink into packages/ and whether that target is required. Decide option (a) reproduce-store vs (b) binary-on-PATH-only BEFORE writing skip-bootstrap code.
- **Working-tree ocx.toml is structurally broken (dangling empty [group])**
  - Missing: The synthesis treats the working-tree ocx.toml diff as 'stripped to shfmt+shellcheck, [group.ci] removed' (Q10) but did not note that the working tree contains a dangling, empty `[group]` table header with no entries (verified: `cat ocx.toml` ends with a bare `[group]` line). This may be a parse hazard or no-op; either way it is uncommitted local cruft that the synthesis glossed as a clean 'scope reduction.' HEAD also pins `git-cliff:2.12` (not `:2` as the synthesis's recommended-tools list states) and `shfmt:3`, `shellcheck:0.11`.
  - Close: Decide: discard the working-tree ocx.toml diff (it is broken) and re-derive the resolvable toolchain from HEAD minus the unresolvable packages (bats/hawkeye/actionlint), plus go-task. Verify `ocx lock` succeeds on the chosen set. Reconcile the git-cliff pin (HEAD says 2.12).
- **ocx.lock declaration_hash will not match the restored toolchain**
  - Missing: ocx.lock (untracked) pins ONLY shfmt+shellcheck and carries declaration_hash sha256:d0ebf9... computed from the 2-tool working-tree ocx.toml. The synthesis says 'commit ocx.lock' and 'restore full toolchain' but these conflict: setup-ocx project mode keys its cache on sha256(ocx.lock) and `ocx pull` validates the lock against ocx.toml. If you restore the toolchain in ocx.toml but commit the current 2-tool lock, the declaration_hash mismatches and `ocx pull` will fail or force re-lock in CI.
  - Close: Order of operations: finalize the resolvable [tools] set in ocx.toml FIRST, then run `ocx lock`, THEN commit BOTH ocx.toml and the freshly-regenerated ocx.lock together. Do not commit the current lock as-is.

### Must verify before implementation

1. RESOLVED in this pass (do not re-litigate): real release v0.3.1 publishes an aggregate sha256.sum (803B) AND per-asset .sha256 files; the installers' '{tag}/sha256.sum' default is correct. Archive internal layout for linux musl/gnu is FLAT (ocx at root), already handled by both installers' fallback extraction branch.
2. Run `ocx package exec --help` and confirm it accepts package refs positionally with auto-install; decide explicitly between 'ocx package exec <ref>' (minimal, non-hermetic) and 'ocx run <name>' + [tools] (hermetic, needs lock) for the taskfile rewrite. The synthesis presents only the latter.
3. On a clean OCX_HOME, test whether placing the binary at .../ocx/cli/current/content/bin/ WITHOUT running `ocx package install` yields a self-resolvable install (inspect whether `current` must point into packages/ocx.sh/sha256/... and whether that requires object-store population). This determines whether OCX_INSTALL_SKIP_BOOTSTRAP can ever produce a real-resolvable store.
4. Validate ALL pwsh/install.ps1 behavioral claims on an actual pwsh runner (none exists in this environment) — every ps1 finding is static grep only. Also confirm the Windows *.zip archive internal layout (flat vs nested) before trusting Expand-ZipSafely's binary-locate path; egress was blocked so only dist-manifest was consulted.
5. Before committing: finalize resolvable [tools] in ocx.toml (drop bats/hawkeye/actionlint, add go-task, reconcile git-cliff pin which is 2.12 at HEAD), THEN regenerate ocx.lock via `ocx lock`, THEN commit both together — the current untracked lock (shfmt+shellcheck only, declaration_hash d0ebf9...) will mismatch any restored toolchain and break setup-ocx project-mode `ocx pull`.
6. Update tests/install fixtures+stubs so they actually exercise the corrected contract: assert the exact bootstrap argv (package install --select ocx.sh/ocx/cli:VERSION), add a FLAT-layout archive fixture (current fixture is nested-only), and flip every `ocx/current/bin` path assertion to `ocx/cli/current/content/bin`. Note tests/install/fixtures/ is EMPTY — fixtures are generated by helpers/server.bash, contradicting .claude/rules/testing-bash.md which must also be corrected.
7. Confirm the dangling empty [group] table in the working-tree ocx.toml is intentional/parseable or discard the working-tree diff entirely; verify `ocx lock` and `ocx run` succeed on the chosen toolchain in a clean checkout.
